sanskrit: ते तं भुक्त्वा स्वर्गलोकं विशालंक्षीणे पुण्ये मर्त्यलोकं विशन्ति । एव त्रयीधर्ममनुप्रपन्नागतागतं कामकामा लभन्ते ॥
hindiTranslation: वे उस विशाल स्वर्गलोकके भोगोंको भोगकर पुण्य क्षीण होनेपर मृत्युलोकमें आ जाते हैं ।  इस प्रकार तीनों वेदोंमें कहे हुए सकाम धर्मका आश्रय लिये हुए भोगोंकी कामना करनेवाले मनुष्य आवागमनको प्राप्त होते हैं ॥
englishTranslation: After enjoying the vast heaven-world, they return to the mortal world when their merit is exhausted. Thus, those who follow the ritual path of the three Vedas and seek pleasures gain repeated coming and going.
essence: What is won by wanting is lost by time.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: People who seek heavenly rewards through ritual enjoy those rewards for a while, but when their merit is used up, they return to ordinary life and the cycle continues.
simpleInsight: Short-lived rewards feel satisfying, but they do not end restlessness.
detailedMeaning: On the Kurukshetra field, Krishna is exposing the limits of reward-seeking religion. After describing ritualists who aim for heaven, he says even that highest pleasure is temporary: merit fades, the reward ends, and the seeker returns to mortal life.
modernRelevance: You finally get the promotion, the applause, the bonus trip — and by Monday the hunger is back. Krishna points to the exhaustion behind every reward that depends on accumulation.
translationLiteral: ते (they) / तम् (that) / भुक्त्वा (having enjoyed) / स्वर्गलोकम् (heaven-world) / विशालम् (vast) / क्षीणे (having become exhausted) / पुण्ये (merit) / मर्त्यलोकम् (mortal world) / विशन्ति (enter) / एवम् (thus) / त्रयीधर्मम् (the three-Veda ritual dharma) / अनुप्रपन्नाः (having resorted to) / गतागतम् (coming and going) / कामकामा (desiring pleasures) / लभन्ते (obtain)
insight: It shifts the mind from chasing temporary payoff to seeing the loop beneath it.

quoteVariants.text: Pleasure earned by merit ends. The cycle starts again.
Even heaven ends when merit runs out.
